Explanation: The question can be answered by finding the minimum value in the "age at start date" column, which represents the age when each person became the oldest living president. The lowest age listed is Theodore Roosevelt at 49 years, 241 days (row 14). All other start ages are higher (e.g., Taft at 51 years, 170 days; Grant at 53 years, 95 days; Nixon at 60 years, 13 days). Thus, Theodore Roosevelt was the youngest to hold the status among the entries.
